ask: Ask the structured knowledge base a what / how / why question. The answer is grounded: it comes from distilled concept nodes, relations and cards — with citations — and the fit-gate abstains rather than guessing when the KB doesn't actually cover the question. 'High rigor' tightens the evidence bar for stakes questions.
search: Raw hybrid retrieval (dense vectors + sparse FTS + rerank) over ingested chunks, with scores shown. This is the same kb_search the assistant calls as a tool — use it to judge whether retrieval finds the right passages before blaming distillation.
raw: Random ingested chunks, straight from the store. The place to spot OCR garbage, boilerplate, or mis-sectioned documents before they poison distillation — if the raw text is bad, everything downstream is bad.
nodes: Distilled concept nodes — the 'learnings' the distiller extracted, each with meaning, facets (epistemic status, time frame, trust tier, domain) and provenance back to source chunks. This is what kb_ask reasons over.
edges: Typed relations between nodes (causes, treats, inhibits …) with mechanism and regime, plus meta-links (disagrees_with / alternative_to / context_variant_of) — the window into how reconciliation handled conflicting sources.
cards: Procedure and criteria cards — the 'how' knowledge: step sequences, recognition criteria (must / may / must-not features the fit-gate can rule out on), staging and recommendation grades. Built by distill, refined in place by 'refine'.
sources: The source registry: every ingested document with its trust tier and epistemic regime. Trust here decides how much weight a source's claims get at distillation and adjudication; the 'source' CLI verb can rename, regroup or relicense entries.
adjudication: The node-merge queue: cases where link_to_node found an ambiguous match ('is this the same concept?'). Approving merges them; rejecting keeps them separate. Run the 'adjudicate' operation to let the LM propose resolutions in bulk.
gaps: Questions the KB could not answer (kb_ask returned nothing usable). The research loop reads these to decide what to go and learn next — an empty tab means no unmet demand has been recorded.
bundles: Provenance bundles: each source group becomes a bundle, and scenarios pick which bundles load per session (e.g. 'work' vs 'everything'). Splitting exports each bundle to its own .kdb file; the overlay can be SQLCipher-encrypted.
library: The search-only document library: a big uncurated folder tree indexed lexically (FTS) but NOT distilled — for the research loop to search through. Pick which subfolders are indexed here; 'collection' filters searches by topic folder.
ops: Run maintenance jobs from the browser: ingest new documents, distill chunks into nodes/cards, link and refine the graph, adjudicate merges, and bulk-import the external commonsense/causal datasets (import-conceptnet / import-atomic / import-glucose / import-causenet). One job at a time (they contend on the GPU lease and the KB); output streams below. The Import formats table shows which file types this install can read, and External datasets shows which bulk imports are ready to run.
autopilot: The prioritizer: an ordered list of operations the host runs automatically when idle. After each step it re-checks from the top, so higher entries preempt lower ones — e.g. distilling Vinkona's research drops (bundle 'vinkona') before grinding through the big PDF backlog. It yields whenever a manual job runs or the assistant holds the fast/big LM leases.
settings: The host's config.toml, editable in place: embed endpoint, LM endpoints for distillation, ports, auth token, source folders. Changes save to the file; most take effect on the next job, but port/auth changes need a restart.
import: What this knowledge host can ingest. Point 'sources' (Settings) at folders of documents and run 'ingest'; formats marked ✗ need their parser installed first — the command is shown. The library (search-only) accepts the same formats via 'ingest-library'. Wikipedia arrives as a Kiwix .zim via 'ingest --wikipedia'; Vinkona's research drops are plain .md files exported into a sources folder and distil ahead of everything else via the 'vinkona' bundle.
